parameters.name: number
parameters.label: Number of Pulses
parameters.description: The number of pulses to generate.

parameters.name: interp
parameters.label: Interpolate
parameters.description: You can interpolate the values between pulses using the following function curves:

parameters.name: width
parameters.label: Pulse Width
parameters.description: By default, the pulses are a single sample long, but you can increase the Width so that the pulses are steps to the next pulse.

parameters.name: widthunit
parameters.label: Pulse Width Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.label: Limit Type
parameters.description: Enable the Minimum and Maximum clamping values below with this menu.

parameters.name: min
parameters.label: Minimum
parameters.description: The pulses can be restricted to a Minimum limit. If the Limit Type is Clamp, the graph has additional convenient handles at the minimum for each pulse.

parameters.name: max
parameters.label: Maximum
parameters.description: The pulses can be restricted to a Maximum limit. If the Limit Type is Clamp, the graph has additional convenient handles at the maximum for each pulse.

parameters.name: minspacing
parameters.label: Minimum Spacing
parameters.description: When creating pulses via the second input, the Minimum Spacing parameter will separate out pulses from each other with the minimum distance specified here. The unit of the spacing is controlled by the

parameters.name: cascade
parameters.label: Cascade when Spacing
parameters.description: When minimum spacing between pulses is enabled, cascading will shift pulses by the amount of samples added via the minimum spacing.

parameters.name: outpulse
parameters.label: Outside Pulses
parameters.description: Control how to deal with pulse positions specified outside the operators range.

parameters.name: pulseunit
parameters.label: Pulse Index Unit
parameters.description: Control what unit the in the second input specified values should be interpreted as.

parameters.name: separateoutchan
parameters.label: Separate Output Channels
parameters.description: When toggled on, will create a seperate channel per pulse.

parameters.name: nonadditives
parameters.label: Non-Additives
parameters.description: When enabled will not add up amplitudes of pulses specified on the same sample index.

parameters.name: lastpulse
parameters.label: Last Pulse at Last Sample
parameters.description: In order to set the value at the last sample, the option Last at Last Sample is provided. Otherwise, the last pulse is prior to the last sample.

parameters.name: pulse
parameters.label: Pulse
parameters.description: Sequence of pulse values

parameters.name: pulse0value
parameters.label: Pulse 0
parameters.description: The value of the sequence pulse.

parameters.name: channelname
parameters.label: Channel Names
parameters.description: You can creates many channels with simple patterns like " ", which generates 20 channels from chan1 to chan20. See the section, Common Parameters for a description of this and all Options. See Options

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: Start of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples).

parameters.name: startunit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: end
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: End of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples).

parameters.name: endunit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: The sample rate of the channels, in samples per second. Default:

parameters.name: left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description: The left extend conditions (before/after range).

parameters.name: right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description: The right extend conditions (before/after range).

parameters.name: defval
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: The value used for the Default Value extend condition.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).
